commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 17 July 2026

North American venues dominate the liquidity hierarchy: Nasdaq 100 and NYSE lead decisively, with ILLIQ costs of 0.0052% per $1M and 0.0055% per $1M respectively, underpinned by exceptional comparable volumes of $382.6M and $232.7M. The Asia-Pacific second tier—Tokyo Stock Exchange, Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and Euronext—cluster at ILLIQ levels between 0.0291% and 0.0365% per $1M, though Tokyo's 3.2075% turnover ratio stands notably higher than its peers, suggesting concentrated activity in fewer names. Mid-tier venues Xetra and SIX Swiss Exchange show tighter AR spreads (0.6509% and 0.5638% respectively) than their ILLIQ rankings imply, indicating order-book quality may exceed execution cost expectations in smaller universes. Korea Exchange and London Stock Exchange lag materially: Korea's 1.4172% AR spread and 0.1429% ILLIQ per $1M reflect fragmentation risk, while LSE's 0.0751% ILLIQ cost and 0.7938% spread place it firmly at the bottom despite modest $9.0M comparable volume. For cross-border traders, this ranking reinforces well-known depth advantages in established markets while flagging widening spreads in emerging-market and European alternatives.
